tool.name: patmatdb
tool.summary: scan protein sequences with a local deterministic motif database

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `patmatdb` searches protein sequence records against a local motif database and reports stable motif hits in a table. Epithema v1 keeps the database contract deliberately narrow and local rather than recreating historical database plumbing.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local protein input plus one local TSV motif database. Each non-comment database row must contain `<motif-id><TAB><pattern><TAB><optional-description>`. Pattern syntax matches `fuzzpro`: exact amino-acid residues plus `X` as a single-residue wildcard.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The output is a stable hit table with one row per motif match. Each row reports the source record, motif identifier, pattern text, optional description, 1-based inclusive coordinates, and the matched sequence slice.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema patmatdb`. Validation currently covers a committed two-motif TSV database and a committed protein FASTA fixture through the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release supports only local TSV motif databases and the bounded `fuzzpro` pattern model. It does not integrate with external motif archives, profile libraries, or richer scoring semantics.

artifacts.label: Patmatdb protein FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed protein FASTA fixture used for motif-database searching.

artifacts.label: Patmatdb motif TSV fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed TSV motif database fixture containing two simple protein motifs.

examples.title: Report hits from a local motif database
examples.description: Runs `patmatdb` against a committed protein FASTA fixture and a committed TSV motif database.

expected_outputs.label: Motif database hit table
expected_outputs.description: Two hits are reported from the first fixture record, one for each motif entry in the committed TSV database.
